title: The creative economy finding its global audience
dek: A new generation of studios is exporting ideas—not only aesthetics—from West Africa’s largest city.
author: Amara Okafor
body: On a humid Tuesday in Yaba, three illustrators are building a world that will reach screens thousands of kilometres away. Their studio is small, but its ambition is continental: tell stories rooted in Lagos without sanding away the details that make the city unmistakable.

Across film, music, games and design, Nigerian creators are moving beyond the language of breakthrough moments. They are building durable companies, training new talent and negotiating to keep more ownership of the work they create.
The shift matters because cultural influence is becoming economic infrastructure. The next challenge is ensuring that better access to finance and distribution follows the attention.
